content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: xlite-dev/ffpa-attn
项目名: ffpa-attn
讲义目录: ffpa-attn-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/
当前 HEAD: 882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "ffpa-attn",
  "rationale": "ffpa-attn 是一个面向大 head_dim（D>256）预填充注意力的 GPU kernel 库，核心创新是 Split-D（在 MMA 层做 D 维精细分块），并提供 SDPA/CUDA/Triton/CuTeDSL 四套后端、autograd 反向、torch.compile 兼容、持久化自动调优和复杂构建系统，属于大型项目，因此规划 40 篇讲义、9 个单元。学习顺序遵循「先会用→再懂分发→再读 kernel→最后玩构建/调优/扩展」的递进：第 1-2 单元（入门）让读者跑起来并掌握公共 API 的所有典型用法；第 3 单元打通「公共 API→Backend 配置→Meta 校验/回退→autograd Function→自定义算子」这条贯穿全库的 Python 分发主链路，是理解所有后端的钥匙；第 4-5 单元深入默认的 Triton 后端前向/反向 kernel（Split-D、online softmax、shared-pid 反向、decode 路径、高级开关）；第 6-9 单元（专家）覆盖 CuTeDSL 后端、手写 CUDA 后端与 env.py 构建系统、持久化自动调优与 Ray 多卡、基准测试，以及测试、E2E 集成与二次开发扩展。每篇讲义只解决一个独立问题，依赖关系严格按「主链路先行、kernel 依赖分发层、构建/调优依赖 kernel」组织。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "项目概览与快速上手",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "FFPA 是什么：Split-D 与大 head_dim 注意力",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-what-is-ffpa-split-d.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 FFPA 的定位、要解决的问题（标准 FlashAttention 仅支持 D≤256）、Split-D 的核心思想及其 O(1) SRAM、O(d/4) 寄存器复杂度。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 FFPA 与 SDPA/FlashAttention-2 的关系及差异",
            "理解大 head_dim 带来的 SRAM/寄存器压力以及 Split-D 如何解决",
            "掌握 FFPA 的适用场景与已知局限（N<512 或 D≤256 时不如 SDPA）"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "docs/index.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README Split-D 章节",
            "docs/index.md ffpa-design"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 与 docs/index.md 的 Split-D 章节，用自己的话画出 Q×K^T 与 P×V 在 D 维被切成 16 宽片段的示意，并写一段说明为什么 SRAM 复杂度能降到 O(Br×16)≈O(1)。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "安装、构建与三种构建模式",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-install-and-build-modes.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解从 PyPI 安装、源码 Triton-only 构建、以及用 ENABLE_FFPA_CUDA_IMPL=1 构建可选 CUDA 前向扩展三种模式，以及对应的依赖与产物差异。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "区分三种安装/构建模式及其产物（是否带 ffpa_attn._C）",
            "理解 setup.py 与 env.py 在构建中的职责分工",
            "学会用 tools/build_fast.sh 加速源码构建"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "pyproject.toml",
            "setup.py",
            "tools/build_fast.sh",
            "docs/env.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "setup.py BUILD_CUDA_EXT 分支",
            "env.enable_fwd_cuda_impl",
            "pyproject dependencies"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在本地执行 `pip install -e . --no-build-isolation`（Triton-only）确认能 import ffpa_attn；再阅读 setup.py 找到控制 CUDA 扩展的两个环境变量，写出开启 CUDA 前向扩展的完整命令。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "仓库目录结构与代码地图",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-repo-layout-code-map.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "梳理 src/ffpa_attn（Python 分发与四后端）、csrc/cuffpa（手写 CUDA）、bench、tests、tools、docs 各目录的职责，建立整体代码地图。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "记住 src/ffpa_attn 下 aten/triton/cute/cuda/ray/cli 子包各自对应什么",
            "理解 csrc/cuffpa 手写 CUDA kernel 与 generated/ 代码生成的关系",
            "能在仓库中快速定位某个功能对应的源码位置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/ffpa_attn/__init__.py",
            "docs/env.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "src/ffpa_attn 包结构",
            "csrc/cuffpa 目录",
            "bench、tests、tools"
          ],
          "practice_task": "用树形图画出 src/ffpa_attn 的子包结构，并为每个子包写一句职责说明；再在 csrc/cuffpa/generated/ 中找出某个 head_dim 对应的生成文件并解释其命名规则。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "一行代码替换 SDPA：ffpa_attn_func 与 monkey-patch",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-one-line-sdpa-monkey-patch.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "演示用 `F.scaled_dot_product_attention = ffpa_attn_func` 一行 monkey-patch 接入，并解释 FFPA 不支持的情形如何自动回退到 SDPA（避免递归）。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 ffpa_attn_func 与 SDPA 签名对齐的含义",
            "理解 monkey-patch 后为何要调用 torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention 避免递归",
            "能验证 FFPA 是否真的接管了大 D 的前向"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/ffpa_attn/__init__.py",
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py",
            "tests/test_monkey_patch.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ffpa_attn_func",
            "FFPAAttnMeta.fallback",
            "test_monkey_patch._native_sdpa"
          ],
          "practice_task": "仿照 tests/test_monkey_patch.py，写一段脚本：先 monkey-patch SDPA，分别用 D=128 与 D=512 调用，观察哪个走 FFPA、哪个回退，并打印二者的 max_abs_err。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1",
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "公共 API 与典型使用场景",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "ffpa_attn_func 签名、张量布局与返回",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-ffpa-attn-func-signature-layout.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "逐参数讲解 ffpa_attn_func 的签名、[B,Nh,N,D] 张量布局、dtype 约束（fp16/bf16）、scale 默认值与返回值。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 query/key/value 的 [B,Nh,N,D] 布局与 Nh_q 必须是 Nh_kv 整数倍的约束",
            "理解 scale/is_causal/dropout_p/enable_gqa 参数语义",
            "知道返回 O 的形状与 dtype"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py",
            "docs/index.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ffpa_attn_func 签名与 docstring",
            "docs self-attn 示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 docstring 后，构造 B=1,H=32,N=8192,D=512 的 bf16 q/k/v，调用 ffpa_attn_func 并与 SDPA 对比 max_abs_err，复现 docs/index.md 的 self-attention 示例。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l4"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "自注意力 / 交叉注意力 / 解码注意力",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-self-cross-decode-attention.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 Nq 与 Nkv 可不相等带来的交叉注意力与解码（短 query/长 KV）场景，以及 key/value 必须共享 Nkv 的约束。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "区分 self/cross/decode 三种注意力的 Nq 与 Nkv 关系",
            "理解为什么 FFPA 对解码（Nq=1）走专门的 split-KV 路径",
            "能正确构造短 query/长 KV 的输入"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "docs/index.md",
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ffpa_attn_func cross/decode 说明",
            "docs cross-attn 示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "复现 docs/index.md 的 cross-attention 示例（Nq=128,Nkv=8192,D=512），并额外测一个 Nq=1 的解码用例，对比 FFPA 与 SDPA 的输出。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "因果掩码 is_causal 与可加 attn_mask",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-causal-and-attn-mask.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 is_causal 的「query 对齐到 KV 尾部」语义（需 Nkv>=Nq），以及 attn_mask 的布尔/可加两种语义和广播规则。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 is_causal 下 query 行 r 只看 k<=r+(Nkv-Nq) 的尾部对齐约定",
            "掌握 attn_mask 的 2/3/4 维广播与布尔→-inf 转换",
            "知道 is_causal 与显式 attn_mask 不能同时设置"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py",
            "src/ffpa_attn/functional.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "normalize_inputs 因果校验",
            "normalize_attn_mask",
            "_validate_attn_mask_shape"
          ],
          "practice_task": "复现 docs 的 causal 示例；再构造一个 [1,1,1,Nkv] 的可加 key 位置偏置，传入 attn_mask 调用 ffpa_attn_func 并与手动加偏置的 SDPA 对比。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "GQA / MQA 分组查询注意力",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-gqa-mqa-grouped-attention.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 enable_gqa 显式开启 GQA/MQA（Nh_q 是 Nh_kv 的整数倍，MQA 是 Nh_kv=1 特例）以及与 SDPA 的 enable_gqa 语义对齐。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 group_size=Nh_q/Nh_kv 与 enable_gqa 默认 False 的设计",
            "掌握 GQA 输入构造与参考实现（repeat_interleave 对齐）",
            "知道为何 enable_gqa=False 但头数不等会报错"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py",
            "src/ffpa_attn/functional.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "enable_gqa 校验",
            "docs gqa 示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "复现 docs 的 GQA 示例（Nh_q=32,Nh_kv=8,D=512）；再构造一个 MQA（Nh_kv=1）用例并验证输出与 repeat_interleave 后的 SDPA 一致。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l5",
          "title": "变长注意力 ffpa_attn_varlen_func",
          "order": 5,
          "filename": "u2-l5-varlen-packed-thd.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 packed THD 变长 API 的签名、cu_seqlens 约定、return_lse，以及它目前仅 CuTeDSL 后端在 SM8x/SM90 大 D 上支持的限制。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 [T,H,D] packed 布局与 cu_seqlens_q/cu_seqlens_k 的 B+1 累计偏移约定",
            "掌握 max_seqlen_q/k 与 return_lse 的用法",
            "知道哪些选项（window_size/softcap/...）会被拒绝"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py",
            "src/ffpa_attn/cute/__init__.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "ffpa_attn_varlen_func",
            "_ffpa_attn_varlen_cute",
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 FFPA 是什么：Split-D 与大 head_dim 注意力

本讲义是 FFPA 项目的入门概念篇，覆盖四个最小模块：大 head_dim 的困难、Split-D 方案、复杂度结论、适用边界。

FFPA（Faster Flash Prefill Attention）是专为**大 head_dim（D ∈ [320, 1024]）**设计的 Flash Prefill Attention，相对 PyTorch SDPA 快 1.5~3 倍。经典 FlashAttention-2 沿序列长度 N 方向分块、每块宽仍为完整 D，SRAM 占用 ∝ (Bᵣ+2Bᵦ)·D，随 D 线性增长；当 D>256 时撑爆单 SM 的 SRAM 预算，故 FA-2 上限止于 256。

核心创新 **Split-D** 指在 **MMA 指令层**对 QKᵀ 与 PV 两次矩阵乘做**精细分块（fine-grained tiling）**：沿 head_dim 方向切成宽 16 的片段，使 SRAM 任意时刻只驻留 16 宽的 Q/K/V 切片。

由此得到两条硬核结论：**SRAM 复杂度 O(Bᵣ×16)≈O(1)**（与 D 无关）；**寄存器复杂度 O(d/4)**（随 D 线性，1/4 折扣来自 MMA fragment 打包）。即 Split-D 把 D 方向的压力从 SRAM 转移到更能扛的寄存器。

适用边界：FFPA 主攻 **prefill + 大 D + 长序列（N≥512）**；当 **D≤256 或 N<512** 时未必比 SDPA 快，会**自动回退（fallback）到 SDPA**，二者是互补而非替代关系。

引入的关键术语：head_dim(D)、SDPA、FlashAttention-2、SRAM/寄存器、MMA、Split-D、V-group、O(1) SRAM 复杂度、O(d/4) 寄存器复杂度、prefill、fallback。
### u1-l2 安装、构建与三种构建模式

本讲讲清 FFPA 的三种落地方式：PyPI 预编译 wheel（零编译）、源码 Triton-only（默认，不生成二进制扩展 `ffpa_attn._C`）、源码 + CUDA 前向扩展（设 `ENABLE_FFPA_CUDA_IMPL=1` 才编译 `_C`）。核心结论：是否编译 `_C` 由 `setup.py` 的 `BUILD_CUDA_EXT` 决定，等于 `(not FFPA_SKIP_CUDA_EXT) and ENV.enable_fwd_cuda_impl()`；默认为 Triton-only，手写 CUDA 仅覆盖前向，反向仍走 Triton/SDPA。职责分工上 `setup.py` 决定「要不要编」，`env.py` 的 `ENV` 类决定「编什么、怎么编」（head_dim 集合、源文件、nvcc 参数、目标 SM）。关键术语：构建隔离与 `--no-build-isolation`、`CUDA_FWD_AVAILABLE` 运行时优雅降级、按 head_dim 拆翻译单元、`FFPA_DEV_HEADDIMS` 子集、`build_fast.sh` 用 ccache shim + `MAX_JOBS` + `FFPA_NVCC_THREADS` 提速；`pyproject.toml` 的 `packages.find` 把 `csrc*` 排除在 Python 包之外，故 Triton-only 产物不含任何 C++ 源码。
### u1-l3 仓库目录结构与代码地图

本讲是 FFPA 源码地图篇。核心结论：仓库呈双轨制——Python 轨 `src/ffpa_attn` 与 CUDA 轨 `csrc/cuffpa` 经 `pyproject.toml` 包发现规则隔离，`csrc*` 不进 wheel，Triton-only 产物无任何 C++ 源码。`src/ffpa_attn` 采用「公共入口 `ffpa_attn_interface.py`（定义 `ffpa_attn_func`）+ 分发层 `functional.py`（Backend 配置类、autograd.Function）+ 六子包」分层：`aten`（SDPA 小 D 回退，D≤256）、`cuda`（手写 CUDA，需 `_C`）、`triton`（默认，前向+反向）、`cute`（CuTeDSL，目录名与对外后端名 `"cutedsl"` 不一致）、`ray`（多卡自动调优）、`cli`（基准）。关键术语：翻译单元 TU、`.cuh` 模板、按 head_dim 代码生成（文件名仅含「存储 dtype+head_dim」，函数名多编码累加器精度，故每 head_dim 2 文件 3 声明，bf16 必须 fp32 累加、无 bf16f16 变体）、pybind 入口 `ffpa_attn._C`。辅助目录 `bench/tests/tools/docs` 不进 wheel，配置疑问查 `docs/env.md`。
### u1-l4 一行代码替换 SDPA：ffpa_attn_func 与 monkey-patch

本讲说明如何用一行 monkey-patch（`F.scaled_dot_product_attention = ffpa_attn_func`）把 FFPA 接入任意使用 SDPA 的现成模型。前提是 `ffpa_attn_func` 与 SDPA 签名对齐（前三个位置参数 q/k/v 加同名关键字）。关键概念：monkey-patch 指运行时替换模块函数；`torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention` 是 SDPA 的底层 C++ 绑定，区别于 Python 符号 `F.scaled_dot_product_attention`。核心结论：`ffpa_attn_func` 内部先调用 `FFPAAttnMeta.fallback()` 做短路判定，默认 Triton 后端下 `D≤256`、`D>1024`、`8≤Nq<512`、`Nkv<512` 任一成立即静默回退原生 SDPA，否则进入 FFPA kernel（判定只看前向、不看反向）。回退分支必须调底层绑定而非被 patch 过的 Python 符号，否则会无限递归——源码以 `# HACK: Avoid recursive for monkey-patch usage.` 标注；测试用「把底层绑定换成抛异常的桩」同时锁定「大 D 走 FFPA」与「回退不递归」。
### u2-l1 ffpa_attn_func 签名、张量布局与返回

本讲聚焦 FFPA 密集注意力公共入口 `ffpa_attn_func` 的正确调用，其签名与 `torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention`（SDPA）对齐：`(query, key, value, attn_mask=None, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False, scale=None, enable_gqa=False, **kwargs)`，末尾 `**kwargs` 承载 FFPA 专有后端配置。函数体仅三步：`fallback` 短路回退到原生 SDPA（调底层绑定以免递归）、`normalize` 做校验归一化、交 `FFPAAttnFunc.apply` 进入 autograd 边界。

核心约束：`query/key/value` 为四维 `[B, Nh, N, D]` 布局；`Nq` 可不等 `Nkv`（cross/decode）；`Nh_q` 必须整除 `Nh_kv`（GQA/MQA），`key/value` 共享相同 `Nh_kv` 与 `Nkv`，三者 `head_dim` 一致；dtype 须为 `fp16` 或 `bf16` 且一致，否则报 `TypeError`；`scale=None` 时默认取 `1/sqrt(D)`；`enable_gqa` 默认 `False` 以匹配 SDPA，头数不等须显式置 `True`；`is_causal=True` 要求 `Nkv>=Nq`。返回单个张量 `O`，形状 `[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]` 与 query 同形、dtype 一致，内容为 `softmax(scale·QKᵀ)V`，与 SDPA 数值接近。本讲引入并供后续承接的关键术语：SDPA、`[B,Nh,N,D]` 布局、GQA/MQA、group_size、enable_gqa、is_causal 尾对齐约定、normalize_inputs、FFPAAttnFunc.apply。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u2-l2
- 文件名: u2-l2-self-cross-decode-attention.md
- 写入路径: ffpa-attn-tutorial/u2-l2-self-cross-decode-attention.md
- 标题: 自注意力 / 交叉注意力 / 解码注意力
- 学习阶段: beginner
- 主题: 讲解 Nq 与 Nkv 可不相等带来的交叉注意力与解码（短 query/长 KV）场景，以及 key/value 必须共享 Nkv 的约束。
- 学习目标: 区分 self/cross/decode 三种注意力的 Nq 与 Nkv 关系；理解为什么 FFPA 对解码（Nq=1）走专门的 split-KV 路径；能正确构造短 query/长 KV 的输入
- 应覆盖的最小模块: ffpa_attn_func cross/decode 说明，docs cross-attn 示例
- 关键源码: docs/index.md，src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py
- 代码实践任务: 复现 docs/index.md 的 cross-attention 示例（Nq=128,Nkv=8192,D=512），并额外测一个 Nq=1 的解码用例，对比 FFPA 与 SDPA 的输出。
- 依赖讲义: u2-l1

---

## 总目标

每篇讲义应该满足：

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 FFPA 是什么：Split-D 与大 head_dim 注意力

本讲义是 FFPA 项目的入门概念篇，覆盖四个最小模块：大 head_dim 的困难、Split-D 方案、复杂度结论、适用边界。

FFPA（Faster Flash Prefill Attention）是专为**大 head_dim（D ∈ [320, 1024]）**设计的 Flash Prefill Attention，相对 PyTorch SDPA 快 1.5~3 倍。经典 FlashAttention-2 沿序列长度 N 方向分块、每块宽仍为完整 D，SRAM 占用 ∝ (Bᵣ+2Bᵦ)·D，随 D 线性增长；当 D>256 时撑爆单 SM 的 SRAM 预算，故 FA-2 上限止于 256。

核心创新 **Split-D** 指在 **MMA 指令层**对 QKᵀ 与 PV 两次矩阵乘做**精细分块（fine-grained tiling）**：沿 head_dim 方向切成宽 16 的片段，使 SRAM 任意时刻只驻留 16 宽的 Q/K/V 切片。

由此得到两条硬核结论：**SRAM 复杂度 O(Bᵣ×16)≈O(1)**（与 D 无关）；**寄存器复杂度 O(d/4)**（随 D 线性，1/4 折扣来自 MMA fragment 打包）。即 Split-D 把 D 方向的压力从 SRAM 转移到更能扛的寄存器。

适用边界：FFPA 主攻 **prefill + 大 D + 长序列（N≥512）**；当 **D≤256 或 N<512** 时未必比 SDPA 快，会**自动回退（fallback）到 SDPA**，二者是互补而非替代关系。

引入的关键术语：head_dim(D)、SDPA、FlashAttention-2、SRAM/寄存器、MMA、Split-D、V-group、O(1) SRAM 复杂度、O(d/4) 寄存器复杂度、prefill、fallback。
### u1-l2 安装、构建与三种构建模式

本讲讲清 FFPA 的三种落地方式：PyPI 预编译 wheel（零编译）、源码 Triton-only（默认，不生成二进制扩展 `ffpa_attn._C`）、源码 + CUDA 前向扩展（设 `ENABLE_FFPA_CUDA_IMPL=1` 才编译 `_C`）。核心结论：是否编译 `_C` 由 `setup.py` 的 `BUILD_CUDA_EXT` 决定，等于 `(not FFPA_SKIP_CUDA_EXT) and ENV.enable_fwd_cuda_impl()`；默认为 Triton-only，手写 CUDA 仅覆盖前向，反向仍走 Triton/SDPA。职责分工上 `setup.py` 决定「要不要编」，`env.py` 的 `ENV` 类决定「编什么、怎么编」（head_dim 集合、源文件、nvcc 参数、目标 SM）。关键术语：构建隔离与 `--no-build-isolation`、`CUDA_FWD_AVAILABLE` 运行时优雅降级、按 head_dim 拆翻译单元、`FFPA_DEV_HEADDIMS` 子集、`build_fast.sh` 用 ccache shim + `MAX_JOBS` + `FFPA_NVCC_THREADS` 提速；`pyproject.toml` 的 `packages.find` 把 `csrc*` 排除在 Python 包之外，故 Triton-only 产物不含任何 C++ 源码。
### u1-l3 仓库目录结构与代码地图

本讲是 FFPA 源码地图篇。核心结论：仓库呈双轨制——Python 轨 `src/ffpa_attn` 与 CUDA 轨 `csrc/cuffpa` 经 `pyproject.toml` 包发现规则隔离，`csrc*` 不进 wheel，Triton-only 产物无任何 C++ 源码。`src/ffpa_attn` 采用「公共入口 `ffpa_attn_interface.py`（定义 `ffpa_attn_func`）+ 分发层 `functional.py`（Backend 配置类、autograd.Function）+ 六子包」分层：`aten`（SDPA 小 D 回退，D≤256）、`cuda`（手写 CUDA，需 `_C`）、`triton`（默认，前向+反向）、`cute`（CuTeDSL，目录名与对外后端名 `"cutedsl"` 不一致）、`ray`（多卡自动调优）、`cli`（基准）。关键术语：翻译单元 TU、`.cuh` 模板、按 head_dim 代码生成（文件名仅含「存储 dtype+head_dim」，函数名多编码累加器精度，故每 head_dim 2 文件 3 声明，bf16 必须 fp32 累加、无 bf16f16 变体）、pybind 入口 `ffpa_attn._C`。辅助目录 `bench/tests/tools/docs` 不进 wheel，配置疑问查 `docs/env.md`。
### u1-l4 一行代码替换 SDPA：ffpa_attn_func 与 monkey-patch

本讲说明如何用一行 monkey-patch（`F.scaled_dot_product_attention = ffpa_attn_func`）把 FFPA 接入任意使用 SDPA 的现成模型。前提是 `ffpa_attn_func` 与 SDPA 签名对齐（前三个位置参数 q/k/v 加同名关键字）。关键概念：monkey-patch 指运行时替换模块函数；`torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention` 是 SDPA 的底层 C++ 绑定，区别于 Python 符号 `F.scaled_dot_product_attention`。核心结论：`ffpa_attn_func` 内部先调用 `FFPAAttnMeta.fallback()` 做短路判定，默认 Triton 后端下 `D≤256`、`D>1024`、`8≤Nq<512`、`Nkv<512` 任一成立即静默回退原生 SDPA，否则进入 FFPA kernel（判定只看前向、不看反向）。回退分支必须调底层绑定而非被 patch 过的 Python 符号，否则会无限递归——源码以 `# HACK: Avoid recursive for monkey-patch usage.` 标注；测试用「把底层绑定换成抛异常的桩」同时锁定「大 D 走 FFPA」与「回退不递归」。
### u2-l1 ffpa_attn_func 签名、张量布局与返回

本讲聚焦 FFPA 密集注意力公共入口 `ffpa_attn_func` 的正确调用，其签名与 `torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention`（SDPA）对齐：`(query, key, value, attn_mask=None, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False, scale=None, enable_gqa=False, **kwargs)`，末尾 `**kwargs` 承载 FFPA 专有后端配置。函数体仅三步：`fallback` 短路回退到原生 SDPA（调底层绑定以免递归）、`normalize` 做校验归一化、交 `FFPAAttnFunc.apply` 进入 autograd 边界。

核心约束：`query/key/value` 为四维 `[B, Nh, N, D]` 布局；`Nq` 可不等 `Nkv`（cross/decode）；`Nh_q` 必须整除 `Nh_kv`（GQA/MQA），`key/value` 共享相同 `Nh_kv` 与 `Nkv`，三者 `head_dim` 一致；dtype 须为 `fp16` 或 `bf16` 且一致，否则报 `TypeError`；`scale=None` 时默认取 `1/sqrt(D)`；`enable_gqa` 默认 `False` 以匹配 SDPA，头数不等须显式置 `True`；`is_causal=True` 要求 `Nkv>=Nq`。返回单个张量 `O`，形状 `[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]` 与 query 同形、dtype 一致，内容为 `softmax(scale·QKᵀ)V`，与 SDPA 数值接近。本讲引入并供后续承接的关键术语：SDPA、`[B,Nh,N,D]` 布局、GQA/MQA、group_size、enable_gqa、is_causal 尾对齐约定、normalize_inputs、FFPAAttnFunc.apply。

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

content.content:
1	"""Public Python interface for FFPA prefill attention.
2	
3	The CUDA and Triton backend packages register forward kernels as
4	``torch.library`` operators under ``torch.ops.ffpa_attn`` so that
5	``torch.compile`` can trace through the forward path as proper custom ops.
6	
7	Backward pass delegates to Triton FFPA backward or PyTorch SDPA backward
8	functions, routing by headdim and user-selected backend.
9	Small-D directly delegates to ``torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention``;
10	large-D forward continues to use the FFPA Triton kernel by default, with a
11	legacy optional CUDA forward backend available only when compiled in.
12	
13	When the caller opts into the CuTeDSL backend (``forward_backend='cutedsl'``)
14	the dispatch goes through the same :class:`FFPAAttnMeta` normalization and
15	:class:`FFPAAttnFunc` autograd boundary as all other backends.
16	:meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.forward` and :meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.backward` route to
17	:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_forward_cute` and
18	:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_backward_cute`, which
19	handle the SDPA ``[B, H, N, D]`` ↔ FA ``[B, N, H, D]`` layout conversion
20	internally. CuTeDSL compatibility constraints are enforced in two layers:
21	
22	- **Tensor-level** (device architecture, head_dim, dtype):
23	  enforced by :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._require_cute_supported`,
24	  now called from :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.normalize` so all callers see
25	  consistent validation before kernel dispatch.
26	- **Kwarg-level** (``dropout_p``, ``attn_mask``): enforced directly in
27	  :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.normalize`, which raises ``NotImplementedError``
28	  for unsupported combinations. The varlen path additionally uses
29	  :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` for the
30	  full FlashAttention-extension kwarg surface.
31	
32	- **Soft (kwarg-level)**: enforced by
33	  :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` at each
34	  entry shim. The dense ``ffpa_attn_func(forward_backend='cutedsl')``
35	  path only forwards ``dropout_p`` and ``attn_mask`` to the helper;
36	  other unsupported kwargs reach the dense path via ``**kwargs`` and
37	  are rejected later by :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.from_kwargs`'s unknown-key
38	  ``TypeError``. The varlen ``ffpa_attn_varlen_func`` path additionally
39	  forwards ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``, ``block_mask``,
40	  ``score_mod``, ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``,
41	  ``num_splits``, ``alibi_slopes`` to the helper. Any non-default
42	  value raises ``NotImplementedError`` with a single consolidated
43	  message naming every offending option — no silent strip-to-default.
44	  Use ``forward_backend='triton'`` when these options are required.
45	
46	For the dense entry, pure hardware/head-dim mismatches fall back to SDPA with a
47	``warning_once`` log on the ``FFPA.ffpa_attn.ffpa_attn_interface``
48	logger when the backend was not explicitly forced. Every other
49	constraint (dtype, ``dropout_p > 0``, explicit
50	``attn_mask``) continues to raise ``NotImplementedError`` / ``TypeError`` /
51	``ValueError``; there is no silent fallback for those.
52	
53	Variable-length (packed THD) attention is exposed via ``ffpa_attn_varlen_func``,
54	which mirrors the FlashAttention varlen surface (``q, k, v, cu_seqlens_q,
55	cu_seqlens_k, max_seqlen_q, max_seqlen_k, ...``) and delegates to
56	:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_varlen_cute`, which
57	dispatches to the CuTeDSL ``ffpa_attn::_varlen_fwd_cute`` autograd-registered
58	torch op. The varlen API is currently CuTeDSL-only on supported SM8x/SM90
59	large-D shapes; other shapes / backends raise ``NotImplementedError``.
60	"""
61	
62	from __future__ import annotations
63	
64	import torch
65	from .functional import FFPAAttnMeta, FFPAAttnFunc, FFPAAttnVarlenFunc
66	from .logger import init_logger
67	
68	logger = init_logger(__name__)
69	
70	
71	def ffpa_attn_func(
72	  query: torch.Tensor,
73	  key: torch.Tensor,
74	  value: torch.Tensor,
75	  attn_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
76	  dropout_p: float = 0.0,
77	  is_causal: bool = False,
78	  scale: float | None = None,
79	  enable_gqa: bool = False,
80	  **kwargs: object,
81	) -> torch.Tensor:
82	  """FFPA: Faster Flash Prefill Attention for large headdims (D > 256).
83	
84	  Signature aligned with ``torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention``.
85	  Dispatches by ``query.dtype`` (fp16 / bf16) and ``acc`` through a single
86	  registered torch op (``torch.ops.ffpa_attn.attn``), keeping the
87	  Python layer minimal and fully compatible with ``torch.compile``.
88	
89	  Supports cross-attention where ``query`` seqlen (``Nq``) differs from
90	  ``key``/``value`` seqlen (``Nkv``) and grouped-query attention where
91	  ``query`` has more heads than ``key``/``value`` (MQA is the
92	  ``Nh_kv == 1`` special case). ``key`` and ``value`` must share the same
93	  ``Nh_kv`` and the same ``Nkv``. Causal masking is supported via
94	  ``is_causal=True`` with queries aligned to the tail of the KV sequence
95	  (``Nkv >= Nq`` required).
96	
97	  Backward pass is supported via :class:`FFPAAttnFunc`. The public API falls
98	  back to SDPA for cases FFPA does not currently support directly (small-D,
99	  ``D > 1024``, and unsupported large-D dropout), and otherwise keeps the
100	  existing FFPA forward plus SDPA/FFPA backward routing. Large-D Triton forward
101	  and backward support explicit additive ``attn_mask`` gradients.
102	  ``forward_backend`` only affects the large-D path and is accepted as an
103	  FFPA-specific keyword inside ``**kwargs`` so the explicit signature remains
104	  aligned with :func:`torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention`.
105	
106	  :param query: Query tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]``; dtype must be
107	      ``torch.float16`` or ``torch.bfloat16`` and match ``key`` / ``value``.
108	  :param key: Key tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D]``; same dtype as
109	      ``query``. ``Nh_q`` must be an integer multiple of ``Nh_kv``
110	      (``group_size = Nh_q / Nh_kv``).
111	  :param value: Value tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D]``; same dtype
112	      as ``query``. ``key`` and ``value`` must share the same ``Nh_kv`` and
113	      ``Nkv``.
114	  :param attn_mask: Optional attention mask broadcastable to
115	      ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, Nkv]``. Boolean masks follow SDPA semantics where
116	      ``True`` means the element participates in attention; floating masks are
117	      additive attention bias. Large-D Triton supports additive mask gradients.
118	      ``forward_backend='cutedsl'`` rejects any non-``None`` ``attn_mask``
119	      with ``NotImplementedError`` (no silent fallback).
120	    :param dropout_p: Dropout probability. Large-D CUDA and Triton implement
121	      SDPA-style attention dropout; unsupported backends route to SDPA, except
122	      that ``forward_backend='cutedsl'`` raises ``NotImplementedError`` for
123	      ``dropout_p > 0`` instead of falling back.
124	  :param is_causal: When ``True``, apply a causal attention mask so that
125	      query row ``r`` only attends to KV positions ``k <= r + (Nkv - Nq)``
126	      (standard ``queries aligned to KV tail`` convention). Requires
127	      ``Nkv >= Nq``. Non-causal tiles pay only one compare-and-branch
128	      per KV tile; diagonal tiles apply a per-fragment -inf mask.
129	  :param scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor applied to ``QK^T``.
130	      Defaults to ``1 / sqrt(D)`` (standard attention scale) when ``None``.
131	  :param enable_gqa: Grouped-query attention mode. Defaults to ``False`` to
132	      match SDPA exactly. When ``False``, the large-D FFPA path requires
133	      ``query`` and ``key``/``value`` to have the same number of heads. Pass
134	      ``True`` to opt into GQA/MQA semantics explicitly.
135	  :param kwargs: FFPA-specific extension kwargs. Supported keys are
136	      ``backend`` (str or Backend instance), ``forward_backend``, and
137	      ``backward_backend``.  ``backend`` is a shorthand that auto-fills both
138	      ``forward_backend`` and ``backward_backend`` with the same config:
139	      ``backend=\"cutedsl\"`` is equivalent to passing
140	      ``forward_backend=CuTeDSLBackend(), backward_backend=CuTeDSLBackend()``.
141	      Explicit ``forward_backend`` / ``backward_backend`` take priority over
142	      ``backend``.  Any other kwarg raises ``TypeError``.
143	
144	  :returns: Output tensor ``O`` with layout ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]``,
145	      filled with the attention output ``softmax(scale * QK^T) V``.
146	
147	  :raises TypeError: if ``query.dtype`` is neither fp16 nor bf16.
148	  :raises ValueError: if the selected backend config is invalid, if ``acc`` is
149	      not one of ``{'f16', 'f32'}``, if
150	      bf16 activations are combined with ``acc='f16'`` (no bf16-acc mma
151	      PTX instruction exists on supported architectures), or if
152	      ``is_causal=True`` is combined with ``Nkv < Nq``.
153	  :raises NotImplementedError: propagated from SDPA or FFPA backends for
154	      unsupported backend-specific combinations.
155	  """
156	  meta = FFPAAttnMeta.from_kwargs(**kwargs)
157	  if meta.fallback(query, key, attn_mask, dropout_p):
158	    # HACK: Avoid recursive for monkey-patch usage.
159	    return torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention(
160	      query,
161	      key,
162	      value,
163	      attn_mask=attn_mask,
164	      dropout_p=dropout_p,
165	      is_causal=is_causal,
166	      scale=scale,
167	      enable_gqa=enable_gqa,
168	    )
169	
170	  meta, query, key, value, attn_bias = meta.normalize(
171	    query,
172	    key,
173	    value,
174	    attn_mask,
175	    dropout_p,
176	    is_causal,
177	    scale,
178	    enable_gqa,
179	  )
180	
181	  return FFPAAttnFunc.apply(query, key, value, attn_bias, meta)
182	
183	
184	def ffpa_attn_varlen_func(
185	  q: torch.Tensor,
186	  k: torch.Tensor,
187	  v: torch.Tensor,
188	  cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
189	  cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor | None,
190	  max_seqlen_q: int,
191	  max_seqlen_k: int,
192	  *,
193	  dropout_p: float = 0.0,
194	  softmax_scale: float | None = None,
195	  causal: bool = False,
196	  enable_gqa: bool = False,
197	  return_lse: bool = False,
198	  **kwargs: object,
199	) -> torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
200	  """FFPA variable-length attention (packed THD, FlashAttention-style).
201	
202	  Signature aligned with Dao-AILab ``flash_attn_varlen_func``. Inputs are
203	  packed THD: ``q`` is ``[T_q, H_q, D]`` and ``k`` / ``v`` are
204	  ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``. Sequence boundaries are described by ``cu_seqlens_q``
205	  and ``cu_seqlens_k`` (int32 CUDA tensors of length ``B+1`` starting at 0).
206	  When ``cu_seqlens_k is None`` it defaults to ``cu_seqlens_q`` (self-attention).
207	
208	  Only the CuTeDSL backend is supported: SM8x/SM90, large 64-aligned head dims,
209	  fp16 / bf16. Any unsupported case raises an
210	  actionable error immediately — there is no silent fallback to dense /
211	  per-sequence paths. Callers needing other shapes / backends should
212	  unpack the batch and call :func:`ffpa_attn_func` per sequence.
213	
214	  :param q: Query tensor of shape ``[T_q, H_q, D]``.
215	  :param k: Key tensor of shape ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``.
216	  :param v: Value tensor of shape ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``.
217	  :param cu_seqlens_q: ``[B+1]`` int32 CUDA tensor; ``cu_seqlens_q[0] == 0``
218	      and ``cu_seqlens_q[-1] == T_q``.
219	  :param cu_seqlens_k: Same convention for keys; defaults to
220	      ``cu_seqlens_q`` if ``None``.
221	  :param max_seqlen_q: Maximum per-sequence query length across the batch.
222	  :param max_seqlen_k: Maximum per-sequence key length across the batch.
223	  :param dropout_p: FlashAttention-compat; must be ``0.0`` (CuTeDSL has no
224	      dropout support).
225	  :param softmax_scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor; defaults to
226	      ``1 / sqrt(D)``.
227	  :param causal: Apply a lower-right (tail-aligned) causal mask.
228	  :param enable_gqa: Opt-in to GQA/MQA (``H_q != H_kv``). When ``False``,
229	      ``H_q`` must equal ``H_kv``.
230	  :param return_lse: When ``True``, also return the log-sum-exp tensor of
231	      shape ``[H_q, T_q]`` in fp32 (CUDA convention).
232	  :param kwargs: Most kwargs are recognized-and-rejected by
233	      :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` — passing a
234	      non-default value for ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``,
235	      ``attention_mask`` / ``attn_mask``, ``block_mask``, ``score_mod``,
236	      ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``, ``num_splits``, or
237	      ``alibi_slopes`` raises ``NotImplementedError`` (see ``:raises:``).
238	      Remaining kwargs are forwarded to the cutedsl wrapper for unsupported
239	      option checking.
240	
241	  :returns: ``out`` of shape ``[T_q, H_q, D]`` if ``return_lse=False``,
242	      otherwise ``(out, lse)``.
243	
244	  :raises NotImplementedError: for unsupported CuTeDSL head dims or hardware,
245	      ``dropout_p > 0``, or any non-default unsupported kwarg:
246	      ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``,
247	      ``attention_mask`` / ``attn_mask``, ``block_mask``, ``score_mod``,
248	      ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``, ``num_splits``,
249	      ``alibi_slopes``.
250	    :raises TypeError: if ``cu_seqlens_*`` is not int32, or if dtype is not
251	      fp16/bf16.
252	  :raises ValueError: for shape mismatches between ``q``/``k``/``v``,
253	      malformed ``cu_seqlens_*``, or ``enable_gqa=False`` with
254	      ``H_q != H_kv``.
255	  """
256	
257	  return FFPAAttnVarlenFunc.apply(
258	    q,
259	    k,
260	    v,
261	    cu_seqlens_q,
262	    cu_seqlens_k,
263	    max_seqlen_q,
264	    max_seqlen_k,
265	    dropout_p=dropout_p,
266	    softmax_scale=softmax_scale,
267	    causal=causal,
268	    enable_gqa=enable_gqa,
269	    return_lse=return_lse,
270	    **kwargs,
271	  )
272	
273	
274	__all__ = ["ffpa_attn_func", "ffpa_attn_varlen_func"]
275

file.content:
"""Public Python interface for FFPA prefill attention.

The CUDA and Triton backend packages register forward kernels as
``torch.library`` operators under ``torch.ops.ffpa_attn`` so that
``torch.compile`` can trace through the forward path as proper custom ops.

Backward pass delegates to Triton FFPA backward or PyTorch SDPA backward
functions, routing by headdim and user-selected backend.
Small-D directly delegates to ``torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention``;
large-D forward continues to use the FFPA Triton kernel by default, with a
legacy optional CUDA forward backend available only when compiled in.

When the caller opts into the CuTeDSL backend (``forward_backend='cutedsl'``)
the dispatch goes through the same :class:`FFPAAttnMeta` normalization and
:class:`FFPAAttnFunc` autograd boundary as all other backends.
:meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.forward` and :meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.backward` route to
:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_forward_cute` and
:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_backward_cute`, which
handle the SDPA ``[B, H, N, D]`` ↔ FA ``[B, N, H, D]`` layout conversion
internally. CuTeDSL compatibility constraints are enforced in two layers:

- **Tensor-level** (device architecture, head_dim, dtype):
  enforced by :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._require_cute_supported`,
  now called from :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.normalize` so all callers see
  consistent validation before kernel dispatch.
- **Kwarg-level** (``dropout_p``, ``attn_mask``): enforced directly in
  :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.normalize`, which raises ``NotImplementedError``
  for unsupported combinations. The varlen path additionally uses
  :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` for the
  full FlashAttention-extension kwarg surface.

- **Soft (kwarg-level)**: enforced by
  :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` at each
  entry shim. The dense ``ffpa_attn_func(forward_backend='cutedsl')``
  path only forwards ``dropout_p`` and ``attn_mask`` to the helper;
  other unsupported kwargs reach the dense path via ``**kwargs`` and
  are rejected later by :meth:`FFPAAttnMeta.from_kwargs`'s unknown-key
  ``TypeError``. The varlen ``ffpa_attn_varlen_func`` path additionally
  forwards ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``, ``block_mask``,
  ``score_mod``, ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``,
  ``num_splits``, ``alibi_slopes`` to the helper. Any non-default
  value raises ``NotImplementedError`` with a single consolidated
  message naming every offending option — no silent strip-to-default.
  Use ``forward_backend='triton'`` when these options are required.

For the dense entry, pure hardware/head-dim mismatches fall back to SDPA with a
``warning_once`` log on the ``FFPA.ffpa_attn.ffpa_attn_interface``
logger when the backend was not explicitly forced. Every other
constraint (dtype, ``dropout_p > 0``, explicit
``attn_mask``) continues to raise ``NotImplementedError`` / ``TypeError`` /
``ValueError``; there is no silent fallback for those.

Variable-length (packed THD) attention is exposed via ``ffpa_attn_varlen_func``,
which mirrors the FlashAttention varlen surface (``q, k, v, cu_seqlens_q,
cu_seqlens_k, max_seqlen_q, max_seqlen_k, ...``) and delegates to
:func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_attn_varlen_cute`, which
dispatches to the CuTeDSL ``ffpa_attn::_varlen_fwd_cute`` autograd-registered
torch op. The varlen API is currently CuTeDSL-only on supported SM8x/SM90
large-D shapes; other shapes / backends raise ``NotImplementedError``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import torch
from .functional import FFPAAttnMeta, FFPAAttnFunc, FFPAAttnVarlenFunc
from .logger import init_logger

logger = init_logger(__name__)


def ffpa_attn_func(
  query: torch.Tensor,
  key: torch.Tensor,
  value: torch.Tensor,
  attn_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
  dropout_p: float = 0.0,
  is_causal: bool = False,
  scale: float | None = None,
  enable_gqa: bool = False,
  **kwargs: object,
) -> torch.Tensor:
  """FFPA: Faster Flash Prefill Attention for large headdims (D > 256).

  Signature aligned with ``torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention``.
  Dispatches by ``query.dtype`` (fp16 / bf16) and ``acc`` through a single
  registered torch op (``torch.ops.ffpa_attn.attn``), keeping the
  Python layer minimal and fully compatible with ``torch.compile``.

  Supports cross-attention where ``query`` seqlen (``Nq``) differs from
  ``key``/``value`` seqlen (``Nkv``) and grouped-query attention where
  ``query`` has more heads than ``key``/``value`` (MQA is the
  ``Nh_kv == 1`` special case). ``key`` and ``value`` must share the same
  ``Nh_kv`` and the same ``Nkv``. Causal masking is supported via
  ``is_causal=True`` with queries aligned to the tail of the KV sequence
  (``Nkv >= Nq`` required).

  Backward pass is supported via :class:`FFPAAttnFunc`. The public API falls
  back to SDPA for cases FFPA does not currently support directly (small-D,
  ``D > 1024``, and unsupported large-D dropout), and otherwise keeps the
  existing FFPA forward plus SDPA/FFPA backward routing. Large-D Triton forward
  and backward support explicit additive ``attn_mask`` gradients.
  ``forward_backend`` only affects the large-D path and is accepted as an
  FFPA-specific keyword inside ``**kwargs`` so the explicit signature remains
  aligned with :func:`torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention`.

  :param query: Query tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]``; dtype must be
      ``torch.float16`` or ``torch.bfloat16`` and match ``key`` / ``value``.
  :param key: Key tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D]``; same dtype as
      ``query``. ``Nh_q`` must be an integer multiple of ``Nh_kv``
      (``group_size = Nh_q / Nh_kv``).
  :param value: Value tensor with layout ``[B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D]``; same dtype
      as ``query``. ``key`` and ``value`` must share the same ``Nh_kv`` and
      ``Nkv``.
  :param attn_mask: Optional attention mask broadcastable to
      ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, Nkv]``. Boolean masks follow SDPA semantics where
      ``True`` means the element participates in attention; floating masks are
      additive attention bias. Large-D Triton supports additive mask gradients.
      ``forward_backend='cutedsl'`` rejects any non-``None`` ``attn_mask``
      with ``NotImplementedError`` (no silent fallback).
    :param dropout_p: Dropout probability. Large-D CUDA and Triton implement
      SDPA-style attention dropout; unsupported backends route to SDPA, except
      that ``forward_backend='cutedsl'`` raises ``NotImplementedError`` for
      ``dropout_p > 0`` instead of falling back.
  :param is_causal: When ``True``, apply a causal attention mask so that
      query row ``r`` only attends to KV positions ``k <= r + (Nkv - Nq)``
      (standard ``queries aligned to KV tail`` convention). Requires
      ``Nkv >= Nq``. Non-causal tiles pay only one compare-and-branch
      per KV tile; diagonal tiles apply a per-fragment -inf mask.
  :param scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor applied to ``QK^T``.
      Defaults to ``1 / sqrt(D)`` (standard attention scale) when ``None``.
  :param enable_gqa: Grouped-query attention mode. Defaults to ``False`` to
      match SDPA exactly. When ``False``, the large-D FFPA path requires
      ``query`` and ``key``/``value`` to have the same number of heads. Pass
      ``True`` to opt into GQA/MQA semantics explicitly.
  :param kwargs: FFPA-specific extension kwargs. Supported keys are
      ``backend`` (str or Backend instance), ``forward_backend``, and
      ``backward_backend``.  ``backend`` is a shorthand that auto-fills both
      ``forward_backend`` and ``backward_backend`` with the same config:
      ``backend=\"cutedsl\"`` is equivalent to passing
      ``forward_backend=CuTeDSLBackend(), backward_backend=CuTeDSLBackend()``.
      Explicit ``forward_backend`` / ``backward_backend`` take priority over
      ``backend``.  Any other kwarg raises ``TypeError``.

  :returns: Output tensor ``O`` with layout ``[B, Nh_q, Nq, D]``,
      filled with the attention output ``softmax(scale * QK^T) V``.

  :raises TypeError: if ``query.dtype`` is neither fp16 nor bf16.
  :raises ValueError: if the selected backend config is invalid, if ``acc`` is
      not one of ``{'f16', 'f32'}``, if
      bf16 activations are combined with ``acc='f16'`` (no bf16-acc mma
      PTX instruction exists on supported architectures), or if
      ``is_causal=True`` is combined with ``Nkv < Nq``.
  :raises NotImplementedError: propagated from SDPA or FFPA backends for
      unsupported backend-specific combinations.
  """
  meta = FFPAAttnMeta.from_kwargs(**kwargs)
  if meta.fallback(query, key, attn_mask, dropout_p):
    # HACK: Avoid recursive for monkey-patch usage.
    return torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention(
      query,
      key,
      value,
      attn_mask=attn_mask,
      dropout_p=dropout_p,
      is_causal=is_causal,
      scale=scale,
      enable_gqa=enable_gqa,
    )

  meta, query, key, value, attn_bias = meta.normalize(
    query,
    key,
    value,
    attn_mask,
    dropout_p,
    is_causal,
    scale,
    enable_gqa,
  )

  return FFPAAttnFunc.apply(query, key, value, attn_bias, meta)


def ffpa_attn_varlen_func(
  q: torch.Tensor,
  k: torch.Tensor,
  v: torch.Tensor,
  cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
  cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor | None,
  max_seqlen_q: int,
  max_seqlen_k: int,
  *,
  dropout_p: float = 0.0,
  softmax_scale: float | None = None,
  causal: bool = False,
  enable_gqa: bool = False,
  return_lse: bool = False,
  **kwargs: object,
) -> torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
  """FFPA variable-length attention (packed THD, FlashAttention-style).

  Signature aligned with Dao-AILab ``flash_attn_varlen_func``. Inputs are
  packed THD: ``q`` is ``[T_q, H_q, D]`` and ``k`` / ``v`` are
  ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``. Sequence boundaries are described by ``cu_seqlens_q``
  and ``cu_seqlens_k`` (int32 CUDA tensors of length ``B+1`` starting at 0).
  When ``cu_seqlens_k is None`` it defaults to ``cu_seqlens_q`` (self-attention).

  Only the CuTeDSL backend is supported: SM8x/SM90, large 64-aligned head dims,
  fp16 / bf16. Any unsupported case raises an
  actionable error immediately — there is no silent fallback to dense /
  per-sequence paths. Callers needing other shapes / backends should
  unpack the batch and call :func:`ffpa_attn_func` per sequence.

  :param q: Query tensor of shape ``[T_q, H_q, D]``.
  :param k: Key tensor of shape ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``.
  :param v: Value tensor of shape ``[T_k, H_kv, D]``.
  :param cu_seqlens_q: ``[B+1]`` int32 CUDA tensor; ``cu_seqlens_q[0] == 0``
      and ``cu_seqlens_q[-1] == T_q``.
  :param cu_seqlens_k: Same convention for keys; defaults to
      ``cu_seqlens_q`` if ``None``.
  :param max_seqlen_q: Maximum per-sequence query length across the batch.
  :param max_seqlen_k: Maximum per-sequence key length across the batch.
  :param dropout_p: FlashAttention-compat; must be ``0.0`` (CuTeDSL has no
      dropout support).
  :param softmax_scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor; defaults to
      ``1 / sqrt(D)``.
  :param causal: Apply a lower-right (tail-aligned) causal mask.
  :param enable_gqa: Opt-in to GQA/MQA (``H_q != H_kv``). When ``False``,
      ``H_q`` must equal ``H_kv``.
  :param return_lse: When ``True``, also return the log-sum-exp tensor of
      shape ``[H_q, T_q]`` in fp32 (CUDA convention).
  :param kwargs: Most kwargs are recognized-and-rejected by
      :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._check_supported_options` — passing a
      non-default value for ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``,
      ``attention_mask`` / ``attn_mask``, ``block_mask``, ``score_mod``,
      ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``, ``num_splits``, or
      ``alibi_slopes`` raises ``NotImplementedError`` (see ``:raises:``).
      Remaining kwargs are forwarded to the cutedsl wrapper for unsupported
      option checking.

  :returns: ``out`` of shape ``[T_q, H_q, D]`` if ``return_lse=False``,
      otherwise ``(out, lse)``.

  :raises NotImplementedError: for unsupported CuTeDSL head dims or hardware,
      ``dropout_p > 0``, or any non-default unsupported kwarg:
      ``window_size``, ``softcap``, ``sink``,
      ``attention_mask`` / ``attn_mask``, ``block_mask``, ``score_mod``,
      ``aux_tensors``, ``seqused_k``, ``block_table``, ``num_splits``,
      ``alibi_slopes``.
    :raises TypeError: if ``cu_seqlens_*`` is not int32, or if dtype is not
      fp16/bf16.
  :raises ValueError: for shape mismatches between ``q``/``k``/``v``,
      malformed ``cu_seqlens_*``, or ``enable_gqa=False`` with
      ``H_q != H_kv``.
  """

  return FFPAAttnVarlenFunc.apply(
    q,
    k,
    v,
    cu_seqlens_q,
    cu_seqlens_k,
    max_seqlen_q,
    max_seqlen_k,
    dropout_p=dropout_p,
    softmax_scale=softmax_scale,
    causal=causal,
    enable_gqa=enable_gqa,
    return_lse=return_lse,
    **kwargs,
  )


__all__ = ["ffpa_attn_func", "ffpa_attn_varlen_func"]

🤖FFPA: Yet another Faster Flash Prefill Attention
with O(1)⚡️GPU SRAM complexity for large headdim🐑

5 6 7 8 9
10 11
12 13 **FFPA(Split-D)**: Yet another **Faster Flash Prefill Attention** with **Split-D** strategy, achieve **O(1) SRAM complexity** and **O(d/4) register complexity** for large headdim (**> 256**), **1.5~3x** 🎉 faster than SDPA. 📚👇The Core features: 14 15
16 17 |[Self Attn](./bench)| [GQA/MQA](./bench) |[Cross Attn](./bench)|[Causal/Mask](./bench)|[Dropout](./bench)|[Headdim](#ffpa-design)|[Fwd/Bwd](./bench)| 18 |:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| 19 |✔️(`Nq=Nkv`)|✔️(`Hq!=Hkv`)|✔️(`Nq!=Nkv`)|✔️(`attn_mask`)|✔️(`p>0`)|**320~1024** |**1.5~3x↑** | 20 21
22 23 ## 🎉🎉 Latest News 24 25 - [2026-06-10] DefTruth, Butterfingrz (2026). [FFPA: Efficient Flash Prefill Attention for Large Head Dimensions via Split-D.](assets/papers/ffpa-tech-report-v1.pdf) Zenodo, 2026. [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.20638547.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20638547) 🎉🎉🎉 26 27 ## 📖 Quick Start 28 29
30 31 First, install the prebuilt package from [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/ffpa-attn/) or build [ffpa-attn](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn) from source: 32 33 ```bash 34 # Fisrt, install the prebuilt package from PyPI 35 pip3 install -U ffpa-attn # CUDA 13.0+, PyTorch 2.11+ 36 # Or, build ffpa-attn from source, just follow the cmds 37 git clone https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn.git 38 # Then, build the wheel package (Triton + CuTeDSL backends) 39 cd ffpa-attn && pip3 install -e . --no-build-isolation 40 # Optional: install ffpa-attn w/ CUDA backend (forward only) 41 ENABLE_FFPA_CUDA_IMPL=1 MAX_JOBS=32 pip3 install -e . 42 ``` 43 44 Then, try to accelerate the attention for large headdim with just one-line of code: 45 46 ```python 47 >>> import torch.nn.functional as F 48 >>> from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 49 >>> # Monkey-patch SDPA to point to FFPA. Every thing that FFPA 50 >>> # does not support will auto fallback to SDPA: D <= 256, etc. 51 >>> F.scaled_dot_product_attention = ffpa_attn_func # one-line code 52 ``` 53 54 For more advanced features, please refer to our online docs at 📘[ffpa-attn.io](https://ffpa-attn.readthedocs.io/en/latest/). 55 56 > [!NOTE] 57 > FFPA supports **cross-attention** where the query seqlen ``Nq`` may differ from the key/value seqlen ``Nkv``, **GQA / MQA** attention where Q has ``Nh_q`` heads and K/V have ``Nh_kv`` heads (requires ``Nh_q % Nh_kv == 0``; group size = ``Nh_q / Nh_kv``), and **causal attention** (pass ``is_causal=True``; queries are aligned to the KV tail, i.e. Q row ``r`` attends to ``k <= r + (Nkv - Nq)``, which requires ``Nkv >= Nq``). K/V must share the same ``Nh_kv`` and ``Nkv``. `enable_gqa` now defaults to `False` to match SDPA exactly, so GQA/MQA usage must pass `enable_gqa=True` explicitly. 58 59 60 61 Minimal usage example — **Self-Attention** (B=1, H=32, N=8192, D=512): 62 ```python 63 import torch 64 import torch.nn.functional as F 65 from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 66 67 # D: 32, 64, ..., 320, ..., 1024 (FA-2 <= 256, FFPA supports up to 1024). 68 B, H, N, D = 1, 32, 8192, 512 # batch_size, num_heads, seq_len, head_dim 69 q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 70 k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 71 v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 72 73 # FFPA self attention; layout follows SDPA: (B, H, N, D). 74 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) # -> torch.Tensor of shape (B, H, N, D) 75 print(out.shape, out.dtype) 76 77 ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v) 78 print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") 79 ``` 80 81 82 83 **Cross-Attention** or **Decoding-Attention** example (short query, long KV cache; `Nq != Nkv`): 84 ```python 85 import torch 86 import torch.nn.functional as F 87 from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 88 89 # Short-query / long-KV, e.g. incremental decoding or cross-attention: 90 # Q: [B, H, Nq, D], K/V: [B, H, Nkv, D]; Nq can differ from Nkv but Nk==Nv required. 91 B, H, D = 1, 8, 512 92 Nq, Nkv = 128, 8192 93 q = torch.randn(B, H, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 94 k = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 95 v = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 96 97 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) # -> (B, H, Nq, D) = (1, 8, 128, 512) 98 print(out.shape, out.dtype) 99 100 ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v) 101 print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") 102 ``` 103 104 105 106 **Grouped-Query / Multi-Query Attention** example (Q has more heads than K/V): 107 ```python 108 import torch 109 import torch.nn.functional as F 110 from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 111 112 # GQA: Q has Nh_q heads, K/V share Nh_kv heads; group_size = Nh_q / Nh_kv. 113 # Typical Llama-3-style 32/8 ratio; MQA is the Nh_kv==1 special case. 114 # FFPA targets large headdim so we use D=512 here (FA-2 tops out at D=256). 115 # enable_gqa defaults to False, so opt into GQA semantics explicitly. 116 B, D, Nq, Nkv = 1, 512, 1024, 4096 117 Nh_q, Nh_kv = 32, 8 # group_size = 4 118 q = torch.randn(B, Nh_q, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 119 k = torch.randn(B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 120 v = torch.randn(B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 121 122 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, enable_gqa=True) # -> (B, Nh_q, Nq, D) = (1, 32, 1024, 512) 123 print(out.shape, out.dtype) 124 125 # Reference: replicate K/V along head dim to match Q's head count. 126 group_size = Nh_q // Nh_kv 127 k_ref = k.repeat_interleave(group_size, dim=1) 128 v_ref = v.repeat_interleave(group_size, dim=1) 129 ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k_ref, v_ref) 130 print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") 131 ``` 132 133 134 135 **Causal Attention** example (self-attention causal; also supports chunked / decoding prefill with `Nkv > Nq`): 136 ```python 137 import torch 138 import torch.nn.functional as F 139 from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 140 141 # Causal self-attention: Q row r attends to k <= r (standard triangular mask). 142 # FFPA is tuned for large headdim, so we keep D=512 as in the self-attn example. 143 B, H, N, D = 1, 8, 4096, 512 144 q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 145 k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 146 v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 147 148 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, is_causal=True) 149 print(out.shape, out.dtype) 150 151 ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, is_causal=True) 152 print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") 153 154 # Chunked / decoding prefill: Nq < Nkv, queries aligned to the KV tail 155 # so Q row r attends to k <= r + (Nkv - Nq). Requires Nkv >= Nq. 156 # This example keeps D=512 so it stays on the FFPA large-D path. For D <= 256, 157 # ffpa_attn_func forwards the inputs directly to SDPA without synthesizing a 158 # causal cross-attention mask for you. 159 Nq, Nkv = 128, 8192 160 q = torch.randn(B, H, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 161 k = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 162 v = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") 163 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, is_causal=True) 164 print(out.shape, out.dtype) # (1, 8, 128, 512) 165 ``` 166 167 168 169 **Backward Pass** example (compare dQ / dK / dV against SDPA): 170 ```python 171 import math 172 import torch 173 import torch.nn.functional as F 174 from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func 175 176 # Focus on a large-headdim case where FFPA is typically used. 177 B, H, N, D = 1, 32, 8192, 512 178 scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(D) 179 180 q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) 181 k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) 182 v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) 183 184 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, scale=scale) 185 out.sum().backward() 186 187 dq = q.grad.detach().clone() 188 dk = k.grad.detach().clone() 189 dv = v.grad.detach().clone() 190 191 q_ref = q.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) 192 k_ref = k.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) 193 v_ref = v.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) 194 out_ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q_ref, k_ref, v_ref, scale=scale) 195 out_ref.sum().backward() 196 197 print(f"dQ vs SDPA dQ max_abs_err={(dq - q_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") 198 print(f"dK vs SDPA dK max_abs_err={(dk - k_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") 199 print(f"dV vs SDPA dV max_abs_err={(dv - v_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") 200 ``` 201 202 ## 📖 Split-D 203 204 205 206 We extend FlashAttention to support large headdim ($D>256$) via **fine-grained tiling** at the **MMA** level for $QK^\top$ and $PV$ matrix multiplication, referred to as **Split-D**. This design keeps SRAM usage fixed at $B_r \times 16$ (with $B_r=B_c$) for Q, K and V, yielding constant SRAM complexity $O(B_r \times 16) \approx O(1)$ and register complexity $O(d/4)$. 207 208
209 210

211 FFPA enables headdim > 256, and outperforms standard SDPA by 1.5~3x🎉. 212

213
214 215 > [!NOTE] 216 > FFPA has been tested on `Ampere`, `Ada`, `Hopper`, and `Blackwell` architectures (e.g., A30, L20, 4090, H200, 5090), achieves `1.5~3×↑🎉` speedup over SDPA. FFPA is mainly design for **prefill** and large headdim, and may not be faster than SDPA for 😈 small sequence length (`N<512`) or small headdim (`D<=256`). 217 218 ## 🎉 Benchmark 219 220 Runnable benchmark are provided under [`bench`](./bench). The performance benchmarks for the NVIDIA L20 (**Ada**), NVIDIA Geforce RTX 5090 (**Blackwell**), NVIDIA H800 PCIE (**Hopper**), NVIDIA H200 SXM (**Hopper**, **CuTeDSL** backend, up to **427** TFLOPS!🎉) with large headdims can be found at [`bench`](./bench). 221 222
223 224
225 226
227 228
229 230 231
232 233 ## 🤖 Backends 234 235 FFPA supports multiple backends for the forward and backward pass, including: [`SDPA`](./bench/) (baseline), [`CUDA`](./bench/) (forward only), [`Triton`](./bench/), and [`CuTeDSL`](./bench/). The `CuTeDSL` backend is currently in early stage and has some constraints, but it can achieve up to `427🎉` TFLOPS on H200! Stay tuned for future updates. 236 237
238 239 |Backend|Arch|Fwd|Bwd|Headdim|Autotune|Speedup|Recommend| 240 |:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| 241 |SDPA|sm>=75|✔|✔|All|❌|**1.0x**🤗|sm>=75| 242 |CUDA|sm>=80|✔|❌|320~1024|❌|**1.5x~3x**🎉|sm80~89,120| 243 |Triton|sm>=80|✔|✔|320~1024|✔|**1.5x~5x**🎉|sm>=80| 244 |CuTeDSL|sm>=80|✔|✔|320~1024|❌|**1.5x~2x**🎉|sm80~89,120| 245 |CuTeDSL|sm90|✔|✔|320~512|❌|**3x~6x**🎉|sm90| 246 247 Special thanks to [Butterfingrz](https://github.com/Butterfingrz) for contributing to the CuTeDSL backend! Awesome work!🎉 248 249
250 251 > [!NOTE] 252 > 🔴 **AMD ROCm/HIP support**: the `Triton` backend (forward + backward) also runs on AMD GPUs through Triton's AMD backend, so no extra build flags are needed -- install ffpa-attn into a ROCm build of PyTorch and `ffpa_attn_func` will dispatch to Triton automatically (`pip3 install -e . --no-build-isolation`). The `CUDA` and `CuTeDSL` backends are NVIDIA-only (they rely on NVIDIA PTX such as MMA, ldmatrix and TMA) and are simply unavailable on AMD. Validated on AMD Instinct MI250X (`gfx90a`, Linux) and Radeon RX 9070 XT (`gfx1201`, Windows). 253 254 How to use different backends for your own scenario? Users can simply pass the Backend configs (SDPABackend, CUDABackend, TritonBackend or CuTeDSLBackend) to [ffpa_attn_func](https://ffpa-attn.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/ffpa_attn/), for example: 255 256 ```python 257 >>> from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func, CuTeDSLBackend 258 >>> # CuTeDSL backend, D=512 scenario, fastest on H200!🎉 259 >>> o = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, backend=CuTeDSLBackend()) 260 ``` 261 262 ## ©️License 263 264 265 266 Apache License 2.0 267 268 ## ©️Citations 269 270 ```BibTeX 271 @misc{deftruth2026ffpa, 272 author = {DefTruth and Butterfingrz}, 273 title = {FFPA: Efficient Flash Prefill Attention for Large Head Dimensions via Split-D}, 274 year = {2026}, 275 publisher = {Zenodo}, 276 version = {v1.0}, 277 doi = {10.5281/zenodo.20638547}, 278 url = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20638547} 279 } 280 ``` 281 282 ## 📖 References 283 284 285 286 - [flash-attention](https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention) 287 - [LeetCUDA](https://github.com/xlite-dev/LeetCUDA) 288 - [flashinfer](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer) 289 - [quack](https://github.com/Dao-AILab/quack) 290 - [cutlass](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass) 291

file.content:
🤖FFPA: Yet another Faster Flash Prefill Attention
with O(1)⚡️GPU SRAM complexity for large headdim🐑


**FFPA(Split-D)**: Yet another **Faster Flash Prefill Attention** with **Split-D** strategy, achieve **O(1) SRAM complexity** and **O(d/4) register complexity** for large headdim (**> 256**), **1.5~3x** 🎉 faster than SDPA. 📚👇The Core features:
|[Self Attn](./bench)| [GQA/MQA](./bench) |[Cross Attn](./bench)|[Causal/Mask](./bench)|[Dropout](./bench)|[Headdim](#ffpa-design)|[Fwd/Bwd](./bench)| |:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| |✔️(`Nq=Nkv`)|✔️(`Hq!=Hkv`)|✔️(`Nq!=Nkv`)|✔️(`attn_mask`)|✔️(`p>0`)|**320~1024** |**1.5~3x↑** |
## 🎉🎉 Latest News - [2026-06-10] DefTruth, Butterfingrz (2026). [FFPA: Efficient Flash Prefill Attention for Large Head Dimensions via Split-D.](assets/papers/ffpa-tech-report-v1.pdf) Zenodo, 2026. [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.20638547.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20638547) 🎉🎉🎉 ## 📖 Quick Start
First, install the prebuilt package from [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/ffpa-attn/) or build [ffpa-attn](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn) from source: ```bash # Fisrt, install the prebuilt package from PyPI pip3 install -U ffpa-attn # CUDA 13.0+, PyTorch 2.11+ # Or, build ffpa-attn from source, just follow the cmds git clone https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn.git # Then, build the wheel package (Triton + CuTeDSL backends) cd ffpa-attn && pip3 install -e . --no-build-isolation # Optional: install ffpa-attn w/ CUDA backend (forward only) ENABLE_FFPA_CUDA_IMPL=1 MAX_JOBS=32 pip3 install -e . ``` Then, try to accelerate the attention for large headdim with just one-line of code: ```python >>> import torch.nn.functional as F >>> from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func >>> # Monkey-patch SDPA to point to FFPA. Every thing that FFPA >>> # does not support will auto fallback to SDPA: D <= 256, etc. >>> F.scaled_dot_product_attention = ffpa_attn_func # one-line code ``` For more advanced features, please refer to our online docs at 📘[ffpa-attn.io](https://ffpa-attn.readthedocs.io/en/latest/). > [!NOTE] > FFPA supports **cross-attention** where the query seqlen ``Nq`` may differ from the key/value seqlen ``Nkv``, **GQA / MQA** attention where Q has ``Nh_q`` heads and K/V have ``Nh_kv`` heads (requires ``Nh_q % Nh_kv == 0``; group size = ``Nh_q / Nh_kv``), and **causal attention** (pass ``is_causal=True``; queries are aligned to the KV tail, i.e. Q row ``r`` attends to ``k <= r + (Nkv - Nq)``, which requires ``Nkv >= Nq``). K/V must share the same ``Nh_kv`` and ``Nkv``. `enable_gqa` now defaults to `False` to match SDPA exactly, so GQA/MQA usage must pass `enable_gqa=True` explicitly. Minimal usage example — **Self-Attention** (B=1, H=32, N=8192, D=512): ```python import torch import torch.nn.functional as F from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func # D: 32, 64, ..., 320, ..., 1024 (FA-2 <= 256, FFPA supports up to 1024). B, H, N, D = 1, 32, 8192, 512 # batch_size, num_heads, seq_len, head_dim q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") # FFPA self attention; layout follows SDPA: (B, H, N, D). out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) # -> torch.Tensor of shape (B, H, N, D) print(out.shape, out.dtype) ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v) print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") ``` **Cross-Attention** or **Decoding-Attention** example (short query, long KV cache; `Nq != Nkv`): ```python import torch import torch.nn.functional as F from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func # Short-query / long-KV, e.g. incremental decoding or cross-attention: # Q: [B, H, Nq, D], K/V: [B, H, Nkv, D]; Nq can differ from Nkv but Nk==Nv required. B, H, D = 1, 8, 512 Nq, Nkv = 128, 8192 q = torch.randn(B, H, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") k = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") v = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) # -> (B, H, Nq, D) = (1, 8, 128, 512) print(out.shape, out.dtype) ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v) print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") ``` **Grouped-Query / Multi-Query Attention** example (Q has more heads than K/V): ```python import torch import torch.nn.functional as F from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func # GQA: Q has Nh_q heads, K/V share Nh_kv heads; group_size = Nh_q / Nh_kv. # Typical Llama-3-style 32/8 ratio; MQA is the Nh_kv==1 special case. # FFPA targets large headdim so we use D=512 here (FA-2 tops out at D=256). # enable_gqa defaults to False, so opt into GQA semantics explicitly. B, D, Nq, Nkv = 1, 512, 1024, 4096 Nh_q, Nh_kv = 32, 8 # group_size = 4 q = torch.randn(B, Nh_q, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") k = torch.randn(B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") v = torch.randn(B, Nh_kv, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, enable_gqa=True) # -> (B, Nh_q, Nq, D) = (1, 32, 1024, 512) print(out.shape, out.dtype) # Reference: replicate K/V along head dim to match Q's head count. group_size = Nh_q // Nh_kv k_ref = k.repeat_interleave(group_size, dim=1) v_ref = v.repeat_interleave(group_size, dim=1) ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k_ref, v_ref) print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") ``` **Causal Attention** example (self-attention causal; also supports chunked / decoding prefill with `Nkv > Nq`): ```python import torch import torch.nn.functional as F from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func # Causal self-attention: Q row r attends to k <= r (standard triangular mask). # FFPA is tuned for large headdim, so we keep D=512 as in the self-attn example. B, H, N, D = 1, 8, 4096, 512 q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, is_causal=True) print(out.shape, out.dtype) ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, is_causal=True) print(f"vs SDPA max_abs_err={(out - ref).abs().max().item():.4e}") # Chunked / decoding prefill: Nq < Nkv, queries aligned to the KV tail # so Q row r attends to k <= r + (Nkv - Nq). Requires Nkv >= Nq. # This example keeps D=512 so it stays on the FFPA large-D path. For D <= 256, # ffpa_attn_func forwards the inputs directly to SDPA without synthesizing a # causal cross-attention mask for you. Nq, Nkv = 128, 8192 q = torch.randn(B, H, Nq, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") k = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") v = torch.randn(B, H, Nkv, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda") out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, is_causal=True) print(out.shape, out.dtype) # (1, 8, 128, 512) ``` **Backward Pass** example (compare dQ / dK / dV against SDPA): ```python import math import torch import torch.nn.functional as F from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func # Focus on a large-headdim case where FFPA is typically used. B, H, N, D = 1, 32, 8192, 512 scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(D) q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) k = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) v = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True) out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, scale=scale) out.sum().backward() dq = q.grad.detach().clone() dk = k.grad.detach().clone() dv = v.grad.detach().clone() q_ref = q.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) k_ref = k.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) v_ref = v.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True) out_ref = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q_ref, k_ref, v_ref, scale=scale) out_ref.sum().backward() print(f"dQ vs SDPA dQ max_abs_err={(dq - q_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") print(f"dK vs SDPA dK max_abs_err={(dk - k_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") print(f"dV vs SDPA dV max_abs_err={(dv - v_ref.grad).abs().max().item():.4e}") ``` ## 📖 Split-D We extend FlashAttention to support large headdim ($D>256$) via **fine-grained tiling** at the **MMA** level for $QK^\top$ and $PV$ matrix multiplication, referred to as **Split-D**. This design keeps SRAM usage fixed at $B_r \times 16$ (with $B_r=B_c$) for Q, K and V, yielding constant SRAM complexity $O(B_r \times 16) \approx O(1)$ and register complexity $O(d/4)$.

FFPA enables headdim > 256, and outperforms standard SDPA by 1.5~3x🎉.

> [!NOTE] > FFPA has been tested on `Ampere`, `Ada`, `Hopper`, and `Blackwell` architectures (e.g., A30, L20, 4090, H200, 5090), achieves `1.5~3×↑🎉` speedup over SDPA. FFPA is mainly design for **prefill** and large headdim, and may not be faster than SDPA for 😈 small sequence length (`N<512`) or small headdim (`D<=256`). ## 🎉 Benchmark Runnable benchmark are provided under [`bench`](./bench). The performance benchmarks for the NVIDIA L20 (**Ada**), NVIDIA Geforce RTX 5090 (**Blackwell**), NVIDIA H800 PCIE (**Hopper**), NVIDIA H200 SXM (**Hopper**, **CuTeDSL** backend, up to **427** TFLOPS!🎉) with large headdims can be found at [`bench`](./bench).



## 🤖 Backends FFPA supports multiple backends for the forward and backward pass, including: [`SDPA`](./bench/) (baseline), [`CUDA`](./bench/) (forward only), [`Triton`](./bench/), and [`CuTeDSL`](./bench/). The `CuTeDSL` backend is currently in early stage and has some constraints, but it can achieve up to `427🎉` TFLOPS on H200! Stay tuned for future updates.
|Backend|Arch|Fwd|Bwd|Headdim|Autotune|Speedup|Recommend| |:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:| |SDPA|sm>=75|✔|✔|All|❌|**1.0x**🤗|sm>=75| |CUDA|sm>=80|✔|❌|320~1024|❌|**1.5x~3x**🎉|sm80~89,120| |Triton|sm>=80|✔|✔|320~1024|✔|**1.5x~5x**🎉|sm>=80| |CuTeDSL|sm>=80|✔|✔|320~1024|❌|**1.5x~2x**🎉|sm80~89,120| |CuTeDSL|sm90|✔|✔|320~512|❌|**3x~6x**🎉|sm90| Special thanks to [Butterfingrz](https://github.com/Butterfingrz) for contributing to the CuTeDSL backend! Awesome work!🎉
> [!NOTE] > 🔴 **AMD ROCm/HIP support**: the `Triton` backend (forward + backward) also runs on AMD GPUs through Triton's AMD backend, so no extra build flags are needed -- install ffpa-attn into a ROCm build of PyTorch and `ffpa_attn_func` will dispatch to Triton automatically (`pip3 install -e . --no-build-isolation`). The `CUDA` and `CuTeDSL` backends are NVIDIA-only (they rely on NVIDIA PTX such as MMA, ldmatrix and TMA) and are simply unavailable on AMD. Validated on AMD Instinct MI250X (`gfx90a`, Linux) and Radeon RX 9070 XT (`gfx1201`, Windows). How to use different backends for your own scenario? Users can simply pass the Backend configs (SDPABackend, CUDABackend, TritonBackend or CuTeDSLBackend) to [ffpa_attn_func](https://ffpa-attn.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/ffpa_attn/), for example: ```python >>> from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func, CuTeDSLBackend >>> # CuTeDSL backend, D=512 scenario, fastest on H200!🎉 >>> o = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, backend=CuTeDSLBackend()) ``` ## ©️License Apache License 2.0 ## ©️Citations ```BibTeX @misc{deftruth2026ffpa, author = {DefTruth and Butterfingrz}, title = {FFPA: Efficient Flash Prefill Attention for Large Head Dimensions via Split-D}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Zenodo}, version = {v1.0}, doi = {10.5281/zenodo.20638547}, url = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20638547} } ``` ## 📖 References - [flash-attention](https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention) - [LeetCUDA](https://github.com/xlite-dev/LeetCUDA) - [flashinfer](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer) - [quack](https://github.com/Dao-AILab/quack) - [cutlass](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass)
